title: cinnamomum mabberleyi
intro: Cinnamomum mabberleyi, also known as Mabberley's camphor, is a species of tree in the family Lauraceae. It is native to Papua New Guinea and has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The leaves and bark of the Cinnamomum mabberleyi plant have been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, cough, and skin infections, and to aid digestion and relieve pain. Herbal preparations of Cinnamomum mabberleyi may include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed orally or used topically to harness its medicinal properties.
